title: Add harness field to Config with load/save and validation

paradigm.rationale: Story 02 adds a single dataclass field plus YAML emit/parse plumbing inside config.py. No new module, no new external boundary, no new HTTP surface. The HarnessName seam is a literal-string contract carried by Config.harness; consumers (Stories 03, 04) import `Config` and `HARNESS_NAMES` from `clauded.config` exactly as the existing graph already does.

produces_seams.name: HarnessName

module_constants.name: HARNESS_NAMES
module_constants.rationale: Single source of truth used by Config.load validation, future CLI Choice, future wizard menu. Plain tuple avoids any runtime dep beyond stdlib.

module_constants.name: HarnessName
module_constants.rationale: Documentation-only alias per architecture.md §4. Config.harness stores `str` for dataclass compatibility; the alias is for type-narrowing in callers that wish to opt in.

config_field.name: harness
config_field.position_in_dataclass: after `forward_env`, before any future opencode-specific fields (Story 05 adds `opencode_version` separately)

persistence_key.rationale: FR3 explicit-when-default — persisted even when value equals default. Disambiguates pre-epic configs from post-epic-default configs and prevents the default from drifting silently.

invariants: INV-1: `Config.harness in HARNESS_NAMES` after a successful `Config.load`. Enforced by Config.load via membership check; ConfigValidationError raised otherwise.
INV-2: `Config.harness == "opencode" ⇒ "opencode" in Config.frameworks` after a successful `Config.load`. Enforced by Config.load AFTER frameworks is parsed.

INV-3: `Config.harness in {"claude-code", "codex"}` always satisfies INV-2 because claude-code and codex are non-configurable defaults present in every config (verified against current `from_wizard` defaults and existing claude-code/codex behaviour). Story 02 does NOT inject these into frameworks; INV-2 simply does not trigger for those harness values.
consumers: Story 03 (wizard) — reads `HARNESS_NAMES`, writes `answers['harness']` consumed by from_wizard
Story 04 (CLI + lima) — uses `HARNESS_NAMES` for click.Choice, reads `Config.harness` in dispatcher
Story 05 — does not consume HarnessName directly (opencode mounts key off frameworks, not harness)

components.name: HARNESS_NAMES module constant
components.responsibility: Single immutable enumeration of valid harness identifiers. Imported by load-time validator and (in later stories) by CLI Choice and wizard menu.

components.name: _validate_harness helper
components.responsibility: Resolve and validate the harness value. Returns 'claude-code' when input is None (AC-005). Raises ConfigValidationError with message listing all of HARNESS_NAMES when value is unknown (AC-006). Raises ConfigValidationError with the literal substring 'clauded --edit' when value == 'opencode' and 'opencode' not in frameworks (AC-007).
components.rationale: Mirrors the existing `_validate_distro` / `_validate_runtime_version` / `_validate_version_pin` pattern. Keeps Config.load() readable and the validation logic individually testable. Returning the resolved value (rather than raising on None) matches `_validate_distro`'s default-substitution pattern.

components.name: Config.harness dataclass field
components.responsibility: Persisted, in-memory representation of the active harness for this project.

components.name: Config.load harness plumbing
components.responsibility: Call _validate_harness(data.get('harness'), frameworks_list) AFTER frameworks list is parsed (see existing line 484). Pass the validated value into the cls(...) constructor.
components.ordering_constraint: Validation runs AFTER frameworks parsing because INV-2 needs the frameworks list. Place the call AFTER `env.get('frameworks')` resolves and BEFORE the `cls(...)` return.

components.name: Config.save harness emission
components.responsibility: Always emit `harness:` as a top-level key in the YAML data dict, regardless of whether the value equals the default. Sibling of `vm`, `mount`, `environment`, `claude`, `ssh`. Position: insert as a top-level key in the `data` dict construction (line 515 area). Recommended placement: between `version` and `vm` so the harness selection is visible at the top of the file.
components.yaml_serialization: Plain string scalar; pyyaml will not quote it. Round-trip via Config.load is byte-stable.

components.name: Config.from_wizard harness pass-through
components.responsibility: Read `answers.get('harness', 'claude-code')` and pass it to `cls(...)`. No validation here; the wizard is trusted (its own validation prevents invalid values being put into the answers dict).
components.rationale: Matches existing pattern: from_wizard accepts trusted answers and constructs Config; Config.load is the validating boundary.

boundary_rules_compliance: Rule 1 (no cross-module imports beyond declared public surfaces): unchanged. Story 02 adds public names (HARNESS_NAMES, HarnessName, Config.harness) all on the existing public Config surface.
Rule 2 (Config is single source of truth): preserved. The harness lives only on Config; no env vars, no fallback chains.
Rule 3 (no HTTP client library): not applicable; story does not call HTTP.
Rule 4 (no test-only adaptations in production code): the validation is functionally required by AC-006/007; not a test shim.
Rule 5 (fragile seams require coordinated updates): not applicable; no duplicated lists touched in this story.
Rule 7 (boy-scout USE_BUILTIN_RIPGREP=0 removal): explicitly NOT in this story (Story 04 architect-1's responsibility per kickoff).
implementation_constraints_compliance: Pre-Alpine-removal targeting: not applicable to config.py changes; vm.distro guards live in provisioner.py (Story 01).
Coverage ≥ 80 %: existing test density + new test_config.py cases will keep config.py well above 80 %.
CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry: required. Section: Changed. Wording: 'Added top-level `harness:` field to .clauded.yaml; existing configs default to claude-code.'

Backward compatibility (NFR1, AC-022): hard requirement. Pre-epic configs lacking `harness:` load with harness='claude-code' silently. Validation does NOT enforce INV-2 for the default value because claude-code is always in frameworks (per INV-3).
Each story leaves codebase green: enforced via TDD plus the verifier's `make check` gate.

test_strategy.framework: pytest with hypothesis available; existing patterns
patterns_to_reuse: isolated-filesystem-yaml: write a YAML fixture to tmp_path, load it, assert. Pattern matches existing test_config.py::test_load_config and similar.
round-trip: from_wizard → save → re-read raw YAML text + load → assert

new_test_cases.name: test_harness_defaults_to_claude_code_when_absent
new_test_cases.shape: Write minimal valid YAML omitting `harness:`; Config.load(path); assert config.harness == 'claude-code'; assert no warnings emitted (caplog at WARNING level is empty for that record).

new_test_cases.name: test_harness_unknown_value_rejected
new_test_cases.shape: Write YAML with `harness: gemini`; assert pytest.raises(ConfigValidationError); assert all three of 'claude-code', 'codex', 'opencode' appear in the message.

new_test_cases.name: test_harness_opencode_without_framework_rejected
new_test_cases.shape: Write YAML with `harness: opencode` and frameworks=['claude-code', 'codex']; assert ConfigValidationError; assert 'clauded --edit' substring in message.

new_test_cases.name: test_harness_claude_code_does_not_require_opencode_framework
new_test_cases.shape: Write YAML with `harness: claude-code` and frameworks=[]; assert load succeeds; assert config.harness == 'claude-code'.

new_test_cases.name: test_harness_codex_does_not_require_opencode_framework
new_test_cases.shape: Write YAML with `harness: codex` and frameworks=[]; assert load succeeds; assert config.harness == 'codex'.

new_test_cases.name: test_harness_save_always_emits_field
new_test_cases.shape: Construct a Config with default harness via from_wizard({'harness': 'claude-code', ...}); save(path); read raw YAML text; assert top-level 'harness: claude-code' line is present.

new_test_cases.name: test_harness_round_trip_opencode
new_test_cases.shape: from_wizard({'harness': 'opencode', 'frameworks': ['opencode'], ...}).save(path); reload; assert harness == 'opencode' and YAML text contains 'harness: opencode'.

new_test_cases.name: test_pre_epic_config_loads_clean
new_test_cases.shape: Write a pre-epic-style YAML (NO `harness:` line, NO `opencode` in frameworks, NO `versions.opencode`); load; assert config.harness == 'claude-code'; assert no errors and no warnings (caplog records list filtered to WARNING+ for clauded.* loggers is empty for harness-related entries).

new_test_cases.name: test_harness_round_trip_byte_stability
new_test_cases.shape: Save → load → save again; assert second YAML text is byte-identical to the first (sanity check that no normalization drift happens on resave).

test_fixtures_introduced: Pre-epic-style YAML fixture (constructed inline in the test; no shared fixture file). Mirrors existing tests' inline-YAML pattern.
patterns_to_avoid: Do NOT create conftest.py (project rule: no conftest.py).
Do NOT mock pyyaml — the tests round-trip through real YAML serialization.

implementation_plan_outline: Step 1 (TDD red): write failing tests in tests/test_config.py for AC-005, AC-006, AC-007 (negative + 2 positive variants), AC-008 (always-emit + round-trip), AC-022, plus the byte-stability sanity check. Run pytest; confirm they fail with the right error mode (AttributeError for missing field, etc.).

Step 2 (constants + dataclass field): add HARNESS_NAMES tuple and HarnessName Literal alias near CURRENT_VERSION. Add `harness: str = "claude-code"` to the Config dataclass after `forward_env`. Re-run tests; AC-005 / AC-008 (without validation) should advance.

Step 3 (validator): implement `_validate_harness(value, frameworks)` next to the existing `_validate_*` helpers. Logic: None → 'claude-code'; not in HARNESS_NAMES → ConfigValidationError listing all three; opencode with opencode not in frameworks → ConfigValidationError including 'clauded --edit'. Add a unit-level test for the helper itself (boundary-test the validator) plus the load-level tests already written.
Step 4 (Config.load wiring): call _validate_harness(data.get('harness'), frameworks_value) AFTER the frameworks list is computed; pass the result into cls(harness=...).
Step 5 (Config.save wiring): insert top-level `harness` key into the `data` dict in Config.save BETWEEN `version` and `vm` so the resulting YAML has the harness as the second top-level line.
Step 6 (Config.from_wizard wiring): add `harness=answers.get('harness', 'claude-code')` to the cls(...) call.
Step 7 (CHANGELOG entry): add under [Unreleased] / Changed: 'Added top-level `harness:` field to .clauded.yaml; existing configs default to claude-code.'
Step 8 (lint / typecheck / test gate): make check; ensure ruff, mypy, all 935+ tests green.
Step 9 (cleanup): no temp files; story directory contains only architecture.json, baseline.json, verification.json, result.json.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: INV-2 enforcement breaks AC-022 (a pre-epic config without `harness:` and without 'opencode' in frameworks). Mitigation: _validate_harness returns 'claude-code' for None input, so INV-2 trivially passes (claude-code never triggers the opencode⇒framework rule). Verified by test_pre_epic_config_loads_clean.

YAML key ordering. Mitigation: pyyaml.dump(sort_keys=False) is already used in Config.save; inserting 'harness' between 'version' and 'vm' preserves the deterministic top-down order for newly-saved configs.

from_wizard call sites elsewhere in the codebase pass partial answers dicts that omit 'harness'. Mitigation: from_wizard uses answers.get('harness', 'claude-code') — defaults silently. Existing test_wizard.py tests should still pass without modification (Story 02 introduces the field but the wizard menu lands in Story 03).
Existing test fixtures or factory functions in tests construct Config without `harness=`. Mitigation: dataclass default 'claude-code' covers this.
